review_execution.command: claude -p --model claude-opus-4-8 --effort max --permission-mode dontAsk --tools "" --output-format json < staged diff prompt > /tmp/cp00-112-claude-review-output-valid.json

findings.title: Two taxonomy error codes are declared but never emitted or exercised
findings.evidence: PERMISSION_KERNEL_CP112_INTERFACE_CLOSEOUT_CONTRACT.error_codes (and contract interface_closeout_error_codes) list "permission_denied" and "unauthorized_data_omitted", but createPermissionKernelCp112ApiInterfaceFixture only ever pushes non_synthetic_request_blocked, tenant_boundary_precheck_failed, matter_trace_precheck_failed, and invalid_pagination. No fixture path or test demonstrates the other two; "unauthorized_data_omitted" is also represented as a normal-operation boolean flag (unauthorized_data_omitted: true) rather than an error, so listing it among error codes is semantically mixed.
findings.recommendation: Acceptable for an api_interface_scaffold_only taxonomy that declares forward-looking codes; if desired, add a fixture branch/test exercising permission_denied or annotate these two as scaffold-only/unexercised. Non-blocking.

parsed_review.recommendation: Approve and close CP00-112. The pack is a clean synthetic-only terminal+interface scaffold with no write paths; the single P3 is an informational taxonomy note, not a defect.
parsed_review.rationale: Scope verified item-by-item against the diff. (1) Synthetic-only catalog: PACK_BINDING and contract declare risk_class C, unit_count 150, range RP02.P02.M06.S13-RP02.P03.M05.S06; every catalog row is frozen with synthetic_only/no_real_data/catalog_only and a shared CP112_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION; manifest area_counts (85 service / 65 API) and deliverable_counts sum to 150 and match both the validator and tests. (2) Unauthorized data omission: createPermissionKernelCp112ApiInterfaceFixture builds responseItem via an explicit allow-list (resource_id, resource_type, tenant_id, matter_id, title, annotations) so privileged_note and cross_tenant_secret are never serialized; omitted_fields documents them and tests + validator assert items[0].privileged_note/cross_tenant_secret === undefined. (3) Validation error mapping: non-synthetic, tenant drift, matter drift, and out-of-range page_size each map to explicit codes, and items is emptied ([]) when any error is present. (4) Metadata-only profiles: terminal matrix reuses CP111 profiles and four API fixtures; validator+tests assert idempotency_persisted/lock_acquired/writes_product_state/writes_audit_event all false. (5) No permission/audit/product/database writes: every no_write_attestation flag (mutates_permission_policy, writes_audit_event, writes_product_state, creates_database_rows, persists_idempotency_keys, acquires_locks) is false in both the contract JSON and source; functions are pure, returning frozen objects with no fs/network/DB access; permission_annotation/audit_annotation are preview strings only. (6) No external share/export/AI/LDIP: executes_export_download/external_share/ai_retrieval/claude_review/implements_ldip all false; Hermes command_anchors are inert reference strings; Claude packet is read_only metadata describing the required single review. (7) HRX embedded-only: closeout handoff sets hrx_embedded_boundary=embedded_people_hr_evidence_module_inside_law_firm_os and ldip_implemented=false. (8) Handoff: next_pack_id CP00-113 / next_subphase_id RP02.P03.M05.S07 is consistent across binding, contract, manifest, and handoff, and the CP111 block still chains into CP00-112. Matrix correctness depends on upstream CP111 profile names outside this diff, but the included tests would fail loudly if those profiles were malformed.
notes: No P0/P1/P2 findings were reported by the valid CP00-112 pack-level Claude review.
P3 taxonomy exercise note was documented as non-blocking and carried forward as a CP00-113 API permission/audit binding candidate.
No second valid Claude review was run.
